Contact Rare & Manuscript Collections

Tuol Sleng confessions and photographs, 1991-1993.
Collection Number: 4883

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Tuol Sleng confessions and photographs, 1991-1993.
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
4883
Abstract:
Transcripts of confessions obtained from prisoners at Tuol Sleng Prison, Phnom Penh, Cambodia. Also, five thousand photographs of Tuol Sleng prisoners.
Creator:
Quanitities:
215 microfilm reels.
5 items.
Language:
Collection material in English
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

Transcripts of confessions obtained from prisoners at Tuol Sleng Prison, Phnom Penh, Cambodia. Also, five thousand photographs of Tuol Sleng prisoners.
PROVENANCE

The records in this collection were filmed under the direction of the Cornell University Library's John M. Echols Collection on Southeast Asia and the Department of Conservation and Preservation at the Tuol Sleng museum of Genocide in Phnom Penh, Cambodia, from January 1991 to April 1993. The confessions of some 4300 detainees comprising 400,000 pages of handwritten text in Khmer, as well as internal prison documents were filmed. This project was part of a larger effort to preserve and film Cambodian manuscripts at five locations in Phnom Penh.
RELATED MATERIAL

See also: Guide to Cambodia Documentation Commission Records (collection #4499)
See also: Yale University Cambodian Genocide Program Database for more detailed information about what is on our microfilm. To use this database for materials from this collection, search under "Sources" for "Tuol Sleng Catalogue of Confessions".
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Tuol Sleng Prison Confessions and Photographs, #4883. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
PROVENANCE

The records in this collection were filmed under the direction of the Cornell University Library's John M. Echols Collection on Southeast Asia and the Department of Conservation and Preservation at the Tuol Sleng museum of Genocide in Phnom Penh, Cambodia, from January 1991 to April 1993. The confessions of some 4300 detainees comprising 400,000 pages of handwritten text in Khmer, as well as internal prison documents were filmed. This project was part of a larger effort to preserve and film Cambodian manuscripts at five locations in Phnom Penh.
Biographical / Historical

The records in this collection were filmed under the direction of the Cornell University Library's John M. Echols Collection on Southeast Asia and the Department of Conservation and Preservation at the Tuol Sleng Museum of Genocide in Phnom Penh, Cambodia, from January 1991 to April 1993. The confessions of some 4300 detainees comprising 400,000 pages of handwritten text in Khmer, as well as internal prison documents were filmed. This project was part of a larger effort to preserve and film Cambodian manuscripts at five locations in Phnom Penh.
Restrictions on Use:

For personal research only. Not for commercial use.
Restrictions on Use:

Reproductions may not be made. Originals are owned by the Tuol Sleng Museum of Genocide in Phnom Penh, Cambodia.
SUBJECTS

Names:
Cornell University. Library. Echols Collection
Musée du crime génocide Tuol-sleng
Tuol Sleng (Prison : Phnom Penh, Cambodia)
Places:
Cambodia -- History -- 1975-1979 -- Sources.
